items.prompt: When observe matches the front of the expect queue, you should…
choices: PASS and pop the front expect
FAIL and clear the whole queue
PASS but leave the queue unchanged
items.explain: Match → PASS and pop front; mismatch leaves the queue for debug.

items.prompt: Observing a value when the expect queue is empty should fail.
items.explain: Nothing to compare against — empty-queue observe is a FAIL.
One expect, one matching observe → PASS and pop.

items.prompt: On mismatch you should pop the expect queue to keep moving.
items.explain: Keep the queue on mismatch so you can debug what was expected.

choices: FIFO — order of push matches order of observe
Random access — any expect can match
A single register — only one value ever
items.explain: Scoreboards compare in transaction order — FIFO.
